deep_dive.executive_summary: At $221.89 (2026-07-17 close), the base case yields -33.6% per year over seven years on normalized FY2025 owner cash of $0.25 per share. Bloom Energy is a solid oxide fuel cell manufacturer riding an AI datacenter power narrative: Q1 2026 revenue more than doubled year over year and operating profit was $72 million in a single quarter. Filing-based owner economics remain thin relative to price, with heavy convertible debt, share count up roughly 40% in one year, and South Korea still anchoring the installed base. Marvin proposes watch until price offers a mid-teens annual return on normaliz...

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
applicability_tests: Industry capacity, additions, closures, and replacement costs can be measured.
Current margins can be located relative to a cycle rather than extrapolated.
Maintenance and growth capital are separated.
Leverage and liquidity survive the low case.
failure_modes: replacement cost used for assets that cannot earn their cost of capital

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Operating-only fallback. Add an explicit component_valuation schedule before drawing asset-level conclusions.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Risk and timing are reflected in the low/base/high range.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

insights.title: Why Bloom Energy Stock Soared 248% in the First Half of 2026
insights.claim: Bloom Energy stock surged 248% in H1 2026, driven by major contracts with Oracle (2.8 GW partnership), a $25 billion expansion with Brookfield Asset Management, and strong Q1 earnings with 130% revenue growth and 80% full-year guidance. The company is capitalizing on AI data centers' need for on-site power solutions that bypass traditional grid limitations.

timeline.label: Cash moved +205.7% versus the prior period (802,851.0 to 2,454,108.0).
Operating income moved +217.8% versus the prior period (22,909.0 to 72,802.0).
Leadership / governance risk elevated. BE Investor Notice: Johnson Fistel Investigates Bloom Energy Corporation; 9 executive sales ($14,273,265) in 120d
Q1 2026 revenue $751M (+130% YoY) on surging Energy Server product sales tied to datacenter demand
Reported Q4/FY2025 results: revenue $2.02B (+37%), first full-year operating profit since scale-up phase
Raised ~$1.5B net financing (convertible and debt) boosting cash to $2.45B and long-term recourse debt to $2.6B
Management emphasized AI data center behind-the-meter power and international expansion in risk factors and IR
Launched Bloom Electrolyzer hydrogen platform on shared solid oxide architecture
SK ecoplant partnership scaled South Korea deployments toward 682 MW installed base
Commercialized take-or-pay service and electricity revenue lines alongside product sales
NYSE IPO at $15 per share; began public reporting as pure-play fuel cell manufacturer
Entered South Korea via SK Group distribution partnerships (ecoplant, eternix)
Google/Korea datacenter deployment validated utility-scale Energy Server architecture
Founded by KR Sridhar; developed solid oxide fuel cell stack for NASA Mars program spinout

industry.structure: On-site distributed power is fragmented among natural gas reciprocating engines, turbines, grid imports, batteries, and fuel cells. Bloom is one of few commercial solid oxide fuel cell vendors at multi-megawatt scale; South Korea utility partnerships create a concentrated channel.
industry.share_shift: Bloom is gaining share in datacenter behind-the-meter power where grid interconnection delays and AI load growth favor fast-deploy fuel cells; product revenue rose 208% YoY in Q1 2026. Legacy industrial and Korean utility remain core but U.S. hyperscale orders drove the latest inflection.
industry.trend: AI datacenter buildout, hydrogen electrolyzer pilots, and reliability-focused on-site power are tailwinds; natural gas price, IRA credit rules, and competing microgrid solutions set pricing ceilings.
